root_text.an2.52:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, puggalā loke uppajjamānā uppajjanti bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya, bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.an2.52:1.3: Tathāgato ca arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, rājā ca cakkavattī.
root_text.an2.52:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve puggalā loke uppajjamānā uppajjanti bahujanahitāya bahujanasukhāya, bahuno janassa atthāya hitāya sukhāya devamanussānan”ti.
root_text.an2.53:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, puggalā loke uppajjamānā uppajjanti acchariyamanussā.
root_text.an2.53:1.3: Tathāgato ca arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, rājā ca cakkavattī.
root_text.an2.53:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve puggalā loke uppajjamānā uppajjanti acchariyamanussā”ti.
root_text.an2.54:1.1: “Dvinnaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalānaṁ kālakiriyā bahuno janassa anutappā hoti.
root_text.an2.54:1.3: Tathāgatassa ca arahato sammāsambuddhassa, rañño ca cakkavattissa.
root_text.an2.54:1.4: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ puggalānaṁ kālakiriyā bahuno janassa anutappā hotī”ti.
root_text.an2.55:1.3: Tathāgato ca arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, rājā ca cakkavattī.
root_text.an2.56:1.3: Tathāgato ca arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, paccekabuddho ca.
root_text.an2.57:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve asaniyā phalantiyā na santasantī”ti.
root_text.an2.58:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve asaniyā phalantiyā na santasantī”ti.
root_text.an2.59:1.3: Bhikkhu ca khīṇāsavo, sīho ca migarājā.
root_text.an2.59:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve asaniyā phalantiyā na santasantī”ti.
root_text.an2.60:1.1: “Dveme, bhikkhave, atthavase sampassamānā kiṁpurisā mānusiṁ vācaṁ na bhāsanti.
root_text.an2.60:1.3: Mā ca musā bhaṇimhā, mā ca paraṁ abhūtena abbhācikkhimhāti.
root_text.an2.60:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dve atthavase sampassamānā kiṁpurisā mānusiṁ vācaṁ na bhāsantī”ti.
root_text.an2.61:1.1: “Dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ, bhikkhave, atitto appaṭivāno mātugāmo kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.an2.61:1.4: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ dhammānaṁ atitto appaṭivāno mātugāmo kālaṁ karotī”ti.
root_text.an2.62:1.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an2.62:1.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an2.62:2.1: “Kathañca, bhikkhave, asantasannivāso hoti, kathañca asanto sannivasanti?
root_text.an2.62:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, therassa bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an2.62:2.3: ‘theropi maṁ na vadeyya, majjhimopi maṁ na vadeyya, navopi maṁ na vadeyya;
root_text.an2.62:2.4: therampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ, majjhimampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ, navampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:2.5: Thero cepi maṁ vadeyya ahitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no hitānukampī, noti naṁ vadeyyaṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa nappaṭikareyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:2.6: Majjhimo cepi maṁ vadeyya …pe… navo cepi maṁ vadeyya, ahitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no hitānukampī, noti naṁ vadeyyaṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa nappaṭikareyyaṁ’.
root_text.an2.62:2.7: Majjhimassapi bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti …pe… navassapi bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an2.62:2.8: ‘theropi maṁ na vadeyya, majjhimopi maṁ na vadeyya, navopi maṁ na vadeyya;
root_text.an2.62:2.9: therampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ, majjhimampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ, navampāhaṁ na vadeyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:2.10: Thero cepi maṁ vadeyya ahitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no hitānukampī noti naṁ vadeyyaṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa nappaṭikareyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:2.11: Majjhimo cepi maṁ vadeyya … navo cepi maṁ vadeyya ahitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no hitānukampī, noti naṁ vadeyyaṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa nappaṭikareyyaṁ’.
root_text.an2.62:2.12: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, asantasannivāso hoti, evañca asanto sannivasanti.
root_text.an2.62:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, santasannivāso hoti, kathañca santo sannivasanti?
root_text.an2.62:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, therassa bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an2.62:3.3: ‘theropi maṁ vadeyya, majjhimopi maṁ vadeyya, navopi maṁ vadeyya;
root_text.an2.62:3.4: therampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ, majjhimampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ, navampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:3.5: Thero cepi maṁ vadeyya hitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no ahitānukampī, sādhūti naṁ vadeyyaṁ na naṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa paṭikareyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:3.6: Majjhimo cepi maṁ vadeyya …pe… navo cepi maṁ vadeyya hitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no ahitānukampī, sādhūti naṁ vadeyyaṁ na naṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa paṭikareyyaṁ’.
root_text.an2.62:3.7: Majjhimassapi bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti …pe… navassapi bhikkhuno evaṁ hoti:
root_text.an2.62:3.8: ‘theropi maṁ vadeyya, majjhimopi maṁ vadeyya, navopi maṁ vadeyya;
root_text.an2.62:3.9: therampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ, majjhimampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ, navampāhaṁ vadeyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:3.10: Thero cepi maṁ vadeyya hitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no ahitānukampī, sādhūti naṁ vadeyyaṁ na naṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa paṭikareyyaṁ.
root_text.an2.62:3.11: Majjhimo cepi maṁ vadeyya …pe… navo cepi maṁ vadeyya hitānukampī maṁ vadeyya no ahitānukampī, sādhūti naṁ vadeyyaṁ na naṁ viheṭheyyaṁ passampissa paṭikareyyaṁ’.
root_text.an2.62:3.12: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, santasannivāso hoti, evañca santo sannivasantī”ti.
root_text.an2.63:1.1: “Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe ubhato vacīsaṁsāro diṭṭhipaḷāso cetaso āghāto appaccayo anabhiraddhi ajjhattaṁ avūpasantaṁ hoti, tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an2.63:1.2: ‘dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca na phāsuṁ viharissanti’.
root_text.an2.63:2.1: Yasmiñca kho, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe ubhato vacīsaṁsāro diṭṭhipaḷāso cetaso āghāto appaccayo anabhiraddhi ajjhattaṁ suvūpasantaṁ hoti, tasmetaṁ, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇe pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an2.63:2.2: ‘na dīghattāya kharattāya vāḷattāya saṁvattissati, bhikkhū ca phāsuṁ viharissantī’”ti.

translation_text.an2.52:1.1: “These two individuals, mendicants, arise in the world for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.
translation_text.an2.52:1.3: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha; and the wheel-turning monarch.
translation_text.an2.52:1.4: These are the two individuals who arise in the world for the welfare and happiness of the people, for the people, for the benefit, welfare, and happiness of gods and humans.”
translation_text.an2.53:1.1: “These two individuals, mendicants, arise in the world who are incredible human beings.
translation_text.an2.53:1.3: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha; and the wheel-turning monarch.
translation_text.an2.53:1.4: These are the two individuals who arise in the world who are incredible human beings.”
translation_text.an2.54:1.1: “There are two individuals, mendicants, whose death is regretted by many people.
translation_text.an2.54:1.3: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha; and the wheel-turning monarch.
translation_text.an2.54:1.4: These are the two individuals, mendicants, whose death is regretted by many people.”
translation_text.an2.55:1.1: “Mendicants, these two are worthy of a monument.
translation_text.an2.55:1.3: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha; and the wheel-turning monarch.
translation_text.an2.55:1.4: These are the two who are worthy of a monument.”
translation_text.an2.56:1.1: “Mendicants, there are two kinds of Buddhas.
translation_text.an2.56:1.3: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha; and an Independent Buddha.
translation_text.an2.56:1.4: These are the two kinds of Buddhas.”
translation_text.an2.57:1.1: “These two, mendicants, are not startled by a crack of thunder.
translation_text.an2.57:1.3: A mendicant who has ended defilements; and a thoroughbred elephant.
translation_text.an2.57:1.4: These are the two who are not startled by a crack of thunder.”
translation_text.an2.58:1.1: “These two, mendicants, are not startled by a crack of thunder.
translation_text.an2.58:1.3: A mendicant who has ended defilements; and a thoroughbred horse.
translation_text.an2.58:1.4: These are the two who are not startled by a crack of thunder.”
translation_text.an2.59:1.1: “These two, mendicants, are not startled by a crack of thunder.
translation_text.an2.59:1.3: A mendicant who has ended defilements; and a lion, king of beasts.
translation_text.an2.59:1.4: These are the two who are not startled by a crack of thunder.”
translation_text.an2.60:1.1: “Aborigines see two reasons not to use regular speech.
translation_text.an2.60:1.3: Thinking: ‘May we not lie, and may we not misrepresent others with falsehoods.’
translation_text.an2.60:1.4: Aborigines see these two reasons not to use regular speech.”
translation_text.an2.61:1.1: “Mendicants, ladies die without getting enough of two things.
translation_text.an2.61:1.4: Females die without getting enough of these two things.”
translation_text.an2.62:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you about living with bad people and living with good people.
translation_text.an2.62:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an2.62:2.1: “What is it like living with bad people? How do bad people live together?
translation_text.an2.62:2.2: It’s when a senior mendicant thinks:
translation_text.an2.62:2.3: ‘No mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior, should admonish me;
translation_text.an2.62:2.4: and I shouldn’t admonish any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior.
translation_text.an2.62:2.5: If a mendicant—whether senior, middle, or junior—were to admonish me, they wouldn’t be sympathetic, and I’d bother them by saying “No!” And anyway I wouldn’t deal with it even if I saw what I did wrong.’
translation_text.an2.62:2.7: And a middle or a junior mendicant also thinks:
translation_text.an2.62:2.8: ‘No mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior, should admonish me;
translation_text.an2.62:2.9: and I shouldn’t admonish any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior.
translation_text.an2.62:2.10: If a mendicant—whether senior, middle, or junior—were to admonish me, they wouldn’t be sympathetic, and I’d bother them by saying “No!” And anyway I wouldn’t deal with it even if I saw what I did wrong.’
translation_text.an2.62:2.12: That’s what it’s like living with bad people; that’s how bad people live together.
translation_text.an2.62:3.1: What is it like living with good people? How do good people live together?
translation_text.an2.62:3.2: It’s when a senior mendicant thinks:
translation_text.an2.62:3.3: ‘Any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior, should admonish me;
translation_text.an2.62:3.4: and I should admonish any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior.
translation_text.an2.62:3.5: If a mendicant—whether senior, middle, or junior—were to admonish me, they’d be sympathetic, so I wouldn’t bother them, but say “Thank you!” And I’d deal with it when I saw what I did wrong.’
translation_text.an2.62:3.7: And a middle or a junior mendicant also thinks:
translation_text.an2.62:3.8: ‘Any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior, may admonish me;
translation_text.an2.62:3.9: and I’ll admonish any mendicant, whether senior, middle, or junior.
translation_text.an2.62:3.10: If a mendicant—whether senior, middle, or junior—were to admonish me, they’d be sympathetic, so I wouldn’t bother them, but say “Thank you!” And I’d deal with it when I saw what I did wrong.’
translation_text.an2.62:3.12: That’s what it’s like living with good people; that’s how good people live together.”
translation_text.an2.63:1.1: “In any disciplinary issue where on both sides the endless talk, contempt for each other’s views, resentment, bitterness, and exasperation is not settled, you can expect that this disciplinary issue
translation_text.an2.63:1.2: will lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants will not live comfortably.
translation_text.an2.63:2.1: In any disciplinary issue where on both sides the endless talk, contempt for each other’s views, resentment, bitterness, and exasperation is well settled, you can expect that this disciplinary issue
translation_text.an2.63:2.2: won’t lead to lasting acrimony and enmity, and the mendicants will live comfortably.”

variant_text.an2.62:2.5: viheṭheyyaṁ → viheseyyampi naṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
